methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Brimstone is a public-stage climate and energy company founded by Cody Finke, having raised $100M+. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 comparable companies operating in the Climate & Energy sector: 1 public company (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion.

The peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer density. Of 19 same-sector peers identified, only 2 matched Brimstone's public stage, with 0 peers available at adjacent stages or within similar valuation tiers. This shortfall below the 3-datapoint threshold for reliable median estimation means that an all-sector median would introduce material distortion and is rejected.

Without a viable triangulation method supported by sufficient comparable data, a single-point valuation range cannot be responsibly derived at this time. The analysis is constrained by the narrow peer set available at Brimstone's specific stage and valuation tier within Climate & Energy. Future valuation work should either expand the peer universe through secondary stage peers or await additional comparable public exits in the subsector. Key uncertainties include the company's specific technology positioning within climate solutions and relative competitive standing versus the identified peer group, neither of which can be assessed quantitatively from the available dataset.
